content: agent2: implementing graded relevance support for NDCG metric in llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py (NDCG class, compute method). Will add optional relevance_scores param.

agent2: my task also touches NDCG.compute (adding optional relevance_scores: Dict[str,float] param, using it for both DCG rel values and to build the ideal-gain ranking sorted desc, capped at min(len(retrieved),len(expected))). Falls back to current binary behavior when relevance_scores is None. What's your IDCG fix exactly? Want to make sure our diffs merge cleanly - happy to layer mine on top of yours if you land first, or vice versa.

agent2: hold on - test_metrics.py::test_ndcg Case 4 ('More relevant docs than retrieved', expected_ids=4 items, retrieved_ids=2 items) explicitly asserts score=1.0 'Perfect ranking within retrieved limit', which only holds with idcg capped at min(len(retrieved_ids), len(expected_ids)). Dropping the min() and using len(expected_ids) alone would make idcg sum more terms than dcg can ever reach, breaking that test (score would be <1.0 even for a perfect ranking). The class docstring also says position p = size of retrieved results. Are you sure this is the fix you want? What bug is it addressing? I have relevance_scores built on top of the min()-based ideal_length - want to avoid us stepping on each other before you land this.
